results.title: Water System Guide for DIY Camper Van Conversion - FarOutRide
results.content:
| Shurflo Water Pump | Diaphragm Pump, 3 gallons per minute, 55 PSI | 1 | Amazon |
| Shurflo Accumulator | To reduce cycling | 1 | Amazon |
| Shurflo Strainer | Prevent damaging the pump if crap enters the system… | 1 | Amazon |
| Elbow Adapter | 90° angle to connect Silencer Kit (as needed) | 1 | Amazon |
| Shurflo Silencer Kit | Reduce noise from pump vibration | 1 | Amazon |
| ON/OFF switch | “Emergency” water pump switch | 1 | Amazon |
| Fitting, “Tee”: 1/2″ PEX to 1/2″ PEX to 1/2″ PEX | To split the PEX pipe for sink & shower | 1 | Amazon |
| Valve: 1/2″ PEX | We turn this valve off in winter and drain to prevent freezing (we don’t use the shower or bike wash in winter) | 1 | Amazon |
| SINK AND GREY WATER | [...] Most tanks have 1/2″ NPT female outlet ports, so a 1/2″ NPT male fitting is required (check with your brand/model to make sure).

#### FarOutVan 1

Water Pump Port > Shurflo Silencer Kit > Water Pump

Fresh Water Tank Van Pump Connection

### Drain Port

The outlet used to empty your tank (between trips, winterizing, etc.). It’s usually larger than the other ports, to allow more flow.

### Vent Port

When pumping water out of the tank, or filling it, the water volume removed or taken in must be replaced with air. That’s the role of the vent port. To prevent water from coming out of the vent port (when braking or on steep or rough roads, for example), we connected a hose to the vent port and installed it about 15 inches above the tank. [...] The pressure can be checked with a normal tire gauge (you have one in your glove compartment, right?) and adjusted with a bike pump (it’s a schrader valve), it’s super easy. When checking or adjusting pressure, just remember to turn off the pump and to open the sink faucet (this is to read the static pressure). The accumulator comes unpressurized.

#### Shurflo Accumulator

Buy on Amazon

## Sink

### For Pressurized System
